name: Hydrogen
summary: Hydrogen is the lightest and most abundant element in the universe. It is used in fuel cells, ammonia production and rocket fuel.
funFact: The Sun produces energy by fusing hydrogen into helium.
uses: Fuel cells; Rocket fuel; Ammonia production
personality: Hi! I'm Hydrogen, the lightest element in the universe. I help power the stars!

name: Helium
summary: Helium is an inert noble gas used where non-reactive gases are required.
funFact: Helium makes balloons float and can change your voice.
personality: I'm Helium! I never react and I love making balloons float.

name: Lithium
summary: Lithium is the lightest metal and is essential for rechargeable batteries.
funFact: Most smartphones contain lithium batteries.
personality: I'm Lithium! I store energy for your devices.

name: Beryllium
summary: Beryllium is a light, strong metal used in aerospace.
funFact: Beryllium is transparent to X-rays.
personality: I'm Beryllium! Light but incredibly strong.

name: Boron
summary: Boron is a metalloid used in glass and electronics.
funFact: Boron strengthens borosilicate glass.
personality: I'm Boron! I help make glass tougher.

name: Carbon
summary: Carbon is essential for all known life and forms millions of compounds.
funFact: Diamonds and graphite are both carbon.
personality: I'm Carbon! From diamonds to DNA, I'm everywhere.

name: Nitrogen
summary: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of Earth's atmosphere.
funFact: Most of the air you breathe is nitrogen.
personality: I'm Nitrogen! I quietly fill most of the air.

name: Oxygen
summary: Oxygen is essential for respiration and combustion.
funFact: Liquid oxygen is pale blue.
personality: I'm Oxygen! Most life depends on me.

name: Fluorine
summary: Fluorine is the most reactive chemical element.
funFact: It is the most electronegative element.
personality: I'm Fluorine! I love reacting.

name: Neon
summary: Neon is a noble gas famous for glowing signs.
funFact: Neon glows bright red-orange.
personality: I'm Neon! I light up cities.

name: Sodium
summary: Sodium is a soft, highly reactive alkali metal found naturally in compounds such as table salt.
funFact: Pure sodium reacts vigorously with water.
uses: Table salt; Street lights; Chemical manufacturing
personality: I'm Sodium! Keep me away from water unless you want a spectacular reaction!

name: Magnesium
summary: Magnesium is a lightweight metal essential for plants and humans.
funFact: Magnesium burns with a brilliant white flame.
personality: I'm Magnesium! I help plants make food and create dazzling white flames.

name: Aluminium
summary: Aluminium is a lightweight, corrosion-resistant metal used in transportation and packaging.
funFact: Aluminium is the most abundant metal in Earth's crust.
personality: I'm Aluminium! Light, strong, and everywhere around you.

name: Silicon
summary: Silicon is a metalloid essential for semiconductors and electronics.
funFact: Computer chips are mainly made from silicon.
personality: I'm Silicon! I power the digital world.

name: Phosphorus
summary: Phosphorus is vital for DNA, bones, and energy transfer in living organisms.
funFact: White phosphorus glows faintly in the dark.
personality: I'm Phosphorus! Life depends on me.

name: Sulfur
summary: Sulfur is a yellow nonmetal used in fertilizers and sulfuric acid production.
funFact: Sulfur has been known since ancient times.
personality: I'm Sulfur! I'm bright yellow and incredibly useful.

name: Chlorine
summary: Chlorine is a reactive halogen used for disinfecting water.
funFact: Chlorine keeps many swimming pools safe.
personality: I'm Chlorine! I help keep water clean.

name: Argon
summary: Argon is an inert noble gas commonly used where reactions must be prevented.
funFact: Argon makes up nearly 1% of Earth's atmosphere.
personality: I'm Argon! I stay calm and rarely react.

name: Potassium
summary: Potassium is a soft alkali metal essential for nerve and muscle function.
funFact: Bananas are famous for containing potassium.
personality: I'm Potassium! Your muscles and nerves rely on me.

name: Calcium
summary: Calcium is essential for strong bones, teeth, and many biological processes.
funFact: About 99% of your body's calcium is stored in your bones.
personality: I'm Calcium! I help keep your bones strong.

name: Scandium
summary: Scandium is a lightweight transition metal used in aerospace alloys and sports equipment.
funFact: Scandium is used in high-performance bicycle frames.
uses: Aerospace alloys; Sports equipment; Metal-halide lamps
personality: I'm Scandium! I'm rare, light, and help build high-performance machines.

name: Titanium
summary: Titanium is a strong, lightweight, corrosion-resistant metal widely used in aircraft and medical implants.
funFact: Titanium is as strong as steel but much lighter.
personality: I'm Titanium! Strong, light, and trusted from airplanes to artificial joints.

name: Vanadium
summary: Vanadium strengthens steel and improves its resistance to wear.
funFact: A small amount of vanadium greatly strengthens steel.
personality: I'm Vanadium! I make steel tougher and longer-lasting.

name: Chromium
summary: Chromium is a shiny metal best known for making stainless steel resistant to corrosion.
funFact: Chromium gives emeralds their rich green colour.
personality: I'm Chromium! I keep metal shiny and rust-free.

name: Manganese
summary: Manganese is important in steel production and is essential in small amounts for living organisms.
funFact: Manganese helps plants grow and produce oxygen.
personality: I'm Manganese! I strengthen steel and support life.

name: Iron
summary: Iron is one of the most important metals, forming the basis of steel and carrying oxygen in human blood.
funFact: Most of Earth's core is made primarily of iron.
personality: I'm Iron! I build cities and even help carry oxygen through your body.

name: Cobalt
summary: Cobalt is used in rechargeable batteries and powerful magnets.
funFact: Its name comes from the German word 'kobold', meaning goblin.
personality: I'm Cobalt! I help power electric cars and smartphones.

name: Nickel
summary: Nickel is a corrosion-resistant metal used in coins and stainless steel.
funFact: Many modern coins contain nickel.
personality: I'm Nickel! I'm tough, shiny, and found in your pocket change.

name: Copper
summary: Copper is an excellent conductor of electricity and heat.
funFact: The Statue of Liberty is covered in copper.
personality: I'm Copper! I keep electricity flowing through homes and devices.

name: Zinc
summary: Zinc protects steel from rust and is essential for human health.
funFact: Galvanized steel is coated with zinc to prevent corrosion.
personality: I'm Zinc! I protect metals from rust and help keep you healthy.

name: Gallium
summary: Gallium is a soft metal with a very low melting point used in semiconductors and LEDs.
funFact: Gallium can melt in the palm of your hand.
personality: I'm Gallium! I melt in your hand and help power modern electronics.

name: Germanium
summary: Germanium is a metalloid used in electronics, fibre optics, and infrared devices.
funFact: The first transistors were made using germanium.
personality: I'm Germanium! I helped start the electronics revolution.

name: Arsenic
summary: Arsenic is a naturally occurring metalloid used in semiconductors and some specialized alloys.
funFact: Although poisonous, arsenic has important industrial uses.
personality: I'm Arsenic! Handle me carefully—I can be useful but also dangerous.

name: Selenium
summary: Selenium is an essential trace element used in electronics and glass manufacturing.
funFact: Selenium is important for a healthy immune system.
personality: I'm Selenium! A tiny amount of me helps keep you healthy.

name: Bromine
summary: Bromine is the only nonmetal that is liquid at room temperature.
funFact: Bromine has a deep reddish-brown colour.
personality: I'm Bromine! I'm the only liquid nonmetal.

name: Krypton
summary: Krypton is a noble gas used in lighting and lasers.
funFact: Krypton is named after the Greek word meaning 'hidden'.
personality: I'm Krypton! I glow brilliantly inside special lamps.

name: Rubidium
summary: Rubidium is a very reactive alkali metal used in scientific research.
funFact: Rubidium can ignite spontaneously in air.
personality: I'm Rubidium! I'm extremely reactive and help keep precise time.

name: Strontium
summary: Strontium is an alkaline earth metal famous for producing bright red fireworks.
funFact: Red fireworks get their colour from strontium.
personality: I'm Strontium! I paint the night sky bright red.

name: Yttrium
summary: Yttrium is a transition metal used in LEDs, lasers, and superconductors.
funFact: Yttrium is found in many smartphone components.
personality: I'm Yttrium! I help create brilliant displays and powerful lasers.

name: Zirconium
summary: Zirconium is highly resistant to corrosion and is widely used in nuclear reactors.
funFact: Zirconium absorbs very few neutrons, making it ideal for nuclear reactors.
personality: I'm Zirconium! I'm tough enough for nuclear technology.

name: Niobium
summary: Niobium is a strong, corrosion-resistant transition metal used to strengthen steel alloys.
funFact: Niobium is used in superconducting magnets for MRI scanners.
personality: I'm Niobium! I make steel stronger and power advanced medical technology.

name: Molybdenum
summary: Molybdenum is a tough metal used to strengthen steel and withstand high temperatures.
funFact: Molybdenum helps jet engines survive extreme heat.
personality: I'm Molybdenum! I thrive where the heat is intense.

name: Technetium
summary: Technetium was the first element produced artificially and is radioactive.
funFact: Technetium-99m is widely used in medical imaging.
personality: I'm Technetium! I help doctors see inside the human body.

name: Ruthenium
summary: Ruthenium improves the durability of electrical contacts and alloys.
funFact: Adding ruthenium makes platinum and palladium harder.
personality: I'm Ruthenium! I make precious metals tougher.

name: Rhodium
summary: Rhodium is a rare, highly reflective metal used in catalytic converters.
funFact: Rhodium is often more valuable than gold.
personality: I'm Rhodium! I help reduce vehicle pollution.

name: Palladium
summary: Palladium is a precious metal widely used in catalytic converters and electronics.
funFact: Palladium can absorb large amounts of hydrogen.
personality: I'm Palladium! I clean exhaust gases and love hydrogen.

name: Silver
summary: Silver is the best conductor of electricity among all metals.
funFact: Silver has natural antibacterial properties.
personality: I'm Silver! I conduct electricity better than any other metal.

name: Cadmium
summary: Cadmium is a soft metal used in rechargeable batteries and specialized coatings.
funFact: Cadmium compounds are highly toxic.
personality: I'm Cadmium! Useful in industry, but handle me carefully.

name: Indium
summary: Indium is a soft metal used in touchscreens and LCD displays.
funFact: Your smartphone screen likely contains indium.
personality: I'm Indium! I help make touchscreens work.

name: Tin
summary: Tin is a corrosion-resistant metal commonly used in solder and protective coatings.
funFact: Tin cans are actually steel coated with a thin layer of tin.
personality: I'm Tin! I protect metals and connect electronic circuits.

name: Antimony
summary: Antimony is a brittle metalloid used in flame retardants and lead alloys.
funFact: Ancient civilizations used antimony compounds as cosmetics.
personality: I'm Antimony! I help make materials safer and stronger.

name: Tellurium
summary: Tellurium is a rare metalloid used in solar panels and thermoelectric devices.
funFact: It is rarer than gold in Earth's crust.
personality: I'm Tellurium! I help convert sunlight into electricity.

name: Iodine
summary: Iodine is an essential halogen required for proper thyroid function.
funFact: Iodine vapour is a beautiful violet colour.
personality: I'm Iodine! I help keep your thyroid healthy.

name: Xenon
summary: Xenon is a heavy noble gas used in high-intensity lamps and spacecraft propulsion.
funFact: Some spacecraft use xenon ion engines.
uses: Camera flashes; Ion thrusters; Medical imaging
personality: I'm Xenon! I light up cameras and even power spacecraft.

name: Cesium
summary: Cesium is an extremely reactive alkali metal used in atomic clocks.
funFact: Cesium atomic clocks are accurate to within a second over millions of years.
personality: I'm Cesium! I help keep the world's most accurate time.

name: Barium
summary: Barium is an alkaline earth metal used in medical imaging and drilling fluids.
funFact: Patients drink barium sulfate before some X-ray procedures.
personality: I'm Barium! I help doctors see inside your body.

name: Lanthanum
summary: Lanthanum is the first lanthanide and is used in camera lenses and rechargeable batteries.
funFact: Lanthanum improves the quality of optical glass.
personality: I'm Lanthanum! I make lenses sharper and batteries better.

name: Cerium
summary: Cerium is the most abundant rare-earth element and is used in catalytic converters.
funFact: Cerium oxide is used to polish glass.
personality: I'm Cerium! I help clean the air and polish glass.

name: Praseodymium
summary: Praseodymium is a soft rare-earth metal used in aircraft engines and powerful magnets.
funFact: Praseodymium gives glass a yellow-green colour.
personality: I'm Praseodymium! I strengthen engines and colour glass.

name: Neodymium
summary: Neodymium is famous for making the world's strongest permanent magnets.
funFact: Neodymium magnets are found in headphones and electric motors.
personality: I'm Neodymium! I make incredibly powerful magnets.

name: Promethium
summary: Promethium is a radioactive lanthanide with no stable isotopes.
funFact: It is one of the rarest elements on Earth.
discoverer: Jacob A. Marinsky, Lawrence Glendenin & Charles Coryell
personality: I'm Promethium! I'm rare and radioactive.

name: Samarium
summary: Samarium is used in powerful magnets and nuclear reactors.
funFact: Samarium-cobalt magnets work at high temperatures.
personality: I'm Samarium! I make heat-resistant magnets.

name: Europium
summary: Europium is used in red and blue phosphors for displays.
funFact: Euro banknotes glow under UV because of europium.
personality: I'm Europium! I brighten your screens.

name: Gadolinium
summary: Gadolinium is used as an MRI contrast agent.
funFact: It has unusual magnetic properties.
personality: I'm Gadolinium! I help doctors see inside the body.

name: Terbium
summary: Terbium is used in green phosphors and electronic devices.
funFact: It produces a bright green glow.
personality: I'm Terbium! I add brilliant green light.

name: Dysprosium
summary: Dysprosium improves high-performance magnets.
funFact: Electric vehicles rely on dysprosium-containing magnets.
personality: I'm Dysprosium! I keep powerful magnets working.

name: Holmium
summary: Holmium has one of the highest magnetic strengths.
funFact: Holmium lasers are used in medicine.
personality: I'm Holmium! I'm one of the most magnetic elements.

name: Erbium
summary: Erbium is widely used in fiber-optic communication.
funFact: It amplifies signals in fiber-optic cables.
personality: I'm Erbium! I help the internet travel farther.

name: Thulium
summary: Thulium is one of the least abundant rare-earth elements.
funFact: Portable X-ray machines can contain thulium.
personality: I'm Thulium! I'm rare but useful.

name: Ytterbium
summary: Ytterbium is used in atomic clocks and fiber lasers.
funFact: It helps build ultra-precise clocks.
personality: I'm Ytterbium! I help keep incredibly accurate time.

name: Lutetium
summary: Lutetium is the last lanthanide and is used in PET scanners and catalysts.
funFact: Lutetium is the hardest and densest lanthanide.
personality: I'm Lutetium! I may be rare, but I help doctors diagnose diseases.

name: Hafnium
summary: Hafnium is a corrosion-resistant metal widely used in nuclear reactors.
funFact: Hafnium is excellent at absorbing neutrons.
personality: I'm Hafnium! I help control nuclear reactions.

name: Tantalum
summary: Tantalum is highly corrosion-resistant and used in electronics.
funFact: Many smartphones contain tantalum capacitors.
personality: I'm Tantalum! I keep electronics running reliably.

name: Tungsten
summary: Tungsten has the highest melting point of all pure metals.
funFact: Old incandescent light bulbs used tungsten filaments.
personality: I'm Tungsten! I can handle extreme heat.

name: Rhenium
summary: Rhenium is a rare metal used in jet engines.
funFact: Rhenium improves the lifespan of turbine blades.
discoverer: Walter Noddack, Ida Noddack & Otto Berg
personality: I'm Rhenium! I help aircraft fly higher and faster.

name: Osmium
summary: Osmium is the densest naturally occurring element.
funFact: A cube of osmium is surprisingly heavy for its size.
personality: I'm Osmium! I'm the heavyweight champion of the periodic table.

name: Iridium
summary: Iridium is one of the most corrosion-resistant metals.
funFact: A layer of iridium marks the asteroid impact that contributed to the extinction of the dinosaurs.
personality: I'm Iridium! I survive where most metals fail.

name: Platinum
summary: Platinum is a precious metal used in jewelry and catalytic converters.
funFact: Platinum is rarer than gold.
personality: I'm Platinum! I'm precious, durable, and elegant.

name: Gold
summary: Gold is a valuable metal known for its beauty and resistance to corrosion.
funFact: Almost all the gold on Earth came from ancient neutron star collisions.
personality: I'm Gold! I've been treasured by humans for thousands of years.

name: Mercury
summary: Mercury is the only metal that is liquid at room temperature.
funFact: Mercury expands uniformly with temperature, making it useful in thermometers.
personality: I'm Mercury! I'm the only liquid metal at room temperature.

name: Thallium
summary: Soft, dense post-transition metal used in specialized electronics and research.
funFact: Thallium compounds are highly toxic.
personality: I'm Thallium! Handle me with care.

name: Lead
summary: Dense metal widely used in batteries and radiation shielding.
funFact: Lead has been used since ancient times.
personality: I'm Lead! I'm heavy and dependable.

name: Bismuth
summary: Brittle metal known for colorful crystals and low toxicity.
funFact: Bismuth forms rainbow-colored crystals.
personality: I'm Bismuth! My crystals are beautiful.

name: Polonium
summary: Rare radioactive element discovered by Marie Curie.
funFact: It was named after Poland.
personality: I'm Polonium! I'm powerful and radioactive.

name: Astatine
summary: The rarest naturally occurring halogen.
funFact: Less than one gram exists naturally in Earth's crust at a time.
personality: I'm Astatine! I'm one of Earth's rarest elements.

name: Radon
summary: Radioactive noble gas formed during uranium decay.
funFact: Radon can accumulate in enclosed spaces.
personality: I'm Radon! I'm an invisible radioactive gas.

name: Francium
summary: Extremely rare radioactive alkali metal.
funFact: Only trace amounts occur naturally.
personality: I'm Francium! I'm incredibly rare.

name: Radium
summary: Highly radioactive alkaline earth metal.
funFact: Radium once made glow-in-the-dark paints.
personality: I'm Radium! I made glowing paint famous.

name: Actinium
summary: First member of the actinide series.
funFact: Actinium is intensely radioactive.
personality: I'm Actinium! I begin the actinides.

name: Thorium
summary: Weakly radioactive metal being studied as a nuclear fuel.
funFact: Thorium is more abundant than uranium.
personality: I'm Thorium! I may power future reactors.

name: Protactinium
summary: Protactinium is a rare radioactive actinide found in uranium ores.
funFact: Only tiny amounts occur naturally.
personality: I'm Protactinium! I'm one of the rarest naturally occurring actinides.

name: Uranium
summary: Uranium is a heavy radioactive metal best known as fuel for nuclear reactors.
funFact: Natural uranium powers nuclear energy.
personality: I'm Uranium! I provide enormous amounts of energy.

name: Neptunium
summary: Neptunium was the first transuranium element to be synthesized.
funFact: It is named after the planet Neptune.
personality: I'm Neptunium! I opened the door to synthetic elements.

name: Plutonium
summary: Plutonium is a radioactive element used in nuclear reactors and space missions.
funFact: Some spacecraft use plutonium to generate electricity.
personality: I'm Plutonium! I power missions beyond Earth.

name: Americium
summary: Americium is a synthetic radioactive metal.
funFact: Most household smoke detectors contain americium-241.
personality: I'm Americium! I quietly help keep homes safe.

name: Curium
summary: Curium is a synthetic actinide named after Marie and Pierre Curie.
funFact: Curium glows faintly because of its radioactivity.
personality: I'm Curium! I'm named after two famous scientists.

name: Berkelium
summary: Berkelium is a synthetic radioactive element.
funFact: It was named after Berkeley, California.
personality: I'm Berkelium! I'm a laboratory-made element.

name: Californium
summary: Californium is an intense neutron emitter.
funFact: It is used to start nuclear reactors.
personality: I'm Californium! I produce powerful neutron beams.

name: Einsteinium
summary: Einsteinium is named in honor of Albert Einstein.
funFact: It was discovered after a hydrogen bomb test.
personality: I'm Einsteinium! I'm named after Einstein.

name: Fermium
summary: Fermium is a synthetic radioactive element.
funFact: It is named after Enrico Fermi.
personality: I'm Fermium! I'm named after a pioneer of nuclear physics.

name: Mendelevium
summary: Mendelevium is a synthetic radioactive actinide named after Dmitri Mendeleev.
funFact: Only a few atoms have ever been produced at a time.
personality: I'm Mendelevium! I'm named after the creator of the periodic table.

name: Nobelium
summary: Nobelium is a synthetic radioactive actinide named after Alfred Nobel.
funFact: Its discovery was disputed for many years.
personality: I'm Nobelium! I'm named after the Nobel Prize founder.

name: Lawrencium
summary: Lawrencium is the final actinide and honors Ernest O. Lawrence.
funFact: It marks the end of the actinide series.
personality: I'm Lawrencium! I complete the actinide family.

name: Rutherfordium
summary: Rutherfordium is a synthetic transition metal named after Ernest Rutherford.
funFact: It exists only for a short time before decaying.
personality: I'm Rutherfordium! I'm a tribute to the father of nuclear physics.

name: Dubnium
summary: Dubnium is a synthetic element produced in particle accelerators.
funFact: It is named after Dubna, Russia.
personality: I'm Dubnium! I was created inside a laboratory.

name: Seaborgium
summary: Seaborgium is named after chemist Glenn T. Seaborg.
funFact: It is one of the few elements named after a living scientist.
personality: I'm Seaborgium! I celebrate one of chemistry's greatest scientists.

name: Bohrium
summary: Bohrium is a synthetic element named after physicist Niels Bohr.
funFact: Only a small number of atoms have been created.
personality: I'm Bohrium! I'm named after quantum pioneer Niels Bohr.

name: Hassium
summary: Hassium is a superheavy synthetic element.
funFact: It is named after the German state of Hesse.
personality: I'm Hassium! I'm one of the superheavy elements.

name: Meitnerium
summary: Meitnerium honors physicist Lise Meitner.
funFact: It has only been produced atom by atom.
personality: I'm Meitnerium! I'm named after Lise Meitner.

name: Darmstadtium
summary: Darmstadtium is a synthetic superheavy element.
funFact: It is named after Darmstadt, Germany.
personality: I'm Darmstadtium! I'm one of the newest members of the periodic table.

name: Roentgenium
summary: Roentgenium is a synthetic superheavy transition metal named after Wilhelm Conrad Röntgen, the discoverer of X-rays.
funFact: Only a few atoms of roentgenium have ever been created in laboratories.
personality: I'm Roentgenium! I'm one of the rarest elements ever created.

name: Copernicium
summary: Copernicium is a synthetic superheavy element named after astronomer Nicolaus Copernicus.
funFact: Scientists believe copernicium may behave similarly to a noble gas despite being a metal.
personality: I'm Copernicium! I honor the astronomer who changed our understanding of the Solar System.

name: Nihonium
summary: Nihonium is the first element discovered in Asia and was synthesized by researchers in Japan.
funFact: "Nihon" means "Japan" in Japanese.
personality: I'm Nihonium! I'm the first element discovered in Japan.

name: Flerovium
summary: Flerovium is a synthetic superheavy element named after the Flerov Laboratory of Nuclear Reactions.
funFact: It exists for only fractions of a second before decaying.
personality: I'm Flerovium! I exist only for a very short time.

name: Moscovium
summary: Moscovium is a synthetic superheavy element named after the Moscow region of Russia.
funFact: Only a handful of atoms have ever been produced.
personality: I'm Moscovium! I'm created one atom at a time.

name: Livermorium
summary: Livermorium is a synthetic superheavy element named after Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory in California.
funFact: It is one of the newest elements added to the periodic table.
discoverer: Joint Institute for Nuclear Research & Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory
personality: I'm Livermorium! I'm one of the newest members of the periodic table.

name: Tennessine
summary: Tennessine is a synthetic superheavy halogen named after the U.S. state of Tennessee.
funFact: It was discovered through an international collaboration between scientists from several countries.
discoverer: Joint Institute for Nuclear Research, Oak Ridge National Laboratory, Vanderbilt University & Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory
personality: I'm Tennessine! I'm one of the newest halogens ever discovered.

name: Oganesson
summary: Oganesson is the heaviest known element and currently the final element in the periodic table.
funFact: It is named after physicist Yuri Oganessian, one of the few people to have an element named after them while still alive.
discoverer: Joint Institute for Nuclear Research & Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory
personality: I'm Oganesson! I complete today's periodic table
